name: whatsapp
description: WhatsApp bot (Baileys): write to it and it answers from Elowen AI. Text commands (/model, /new, /help), a per-chat model picker (numbered text menu you reply to with a number), live streaming replies (edit-in-place with the agent's tool trace), status reactions and proactive cron/tick pushes. Group tools (admin sessions): list & inspect groups, create groups, add/remove members, and send a message to any chat (operator). Each sender (phone number, JID or whole group) maps to allowed Elowen projects + a role prompt. Pair by scanning a QR code or with a pairing code.

configSchema.label: Connection
configSchema.hint: How the bot links to WhatsApp and where it pushes proactively. Pair by scanning the QR code from the plugin logs, or with a pairing code.

configSchema.label: Pairing phone number
configSchema.hint: Optional: the bot's WhatsApp number in international format WITHOUT +, spaces or dashes (e.g. 420777123456). When set, the plugin prints an 8-character pairing code to the logs instead of a QR — enter it in WhatsApp → Linked devices → Link with phone number. Leave empty to pair by QR.

configSchema.label: Allowed groups
configSchema.hint: Comma-separated group JIDs (…@g.us). Empty = respond in every group where a mapped sender writes. When set, the bot only responds in these groups. Direct chats are always allowed for mapped senders.

configSchema.label: Notification chat
configSchema.hint: Where proactive messages (cron/tick results, escalations, restart notices) are posted — a phone number or a JID (…@s.whatsapp.net / …@g.us). Empty = no proactive pushes.

configSchema.label: Replies & behavior
configSchema.hint: When and how the bot answers.

configSchema.label: Respond without mention
configSchema.hint: On: in groups the bot answers every message from a mapped sender. Off: in groups it only answers when @mentioned or when its message is replied to. Direct chats always get an answer.

configSchema.label: Live streaming replies
configSchema.hint: Edit the reply in place as it streams (tool calls + text). Off = one message at the end.

configSchema.label: Status reactions
configSchema.hint: React 👀 while thinking, ✅/❌ when done.

configSchema.label: Runtime footer
configSchema.hint: Append a small "model · context %" line under the final reply.

configSchema.label: Show reasoning
configSchema.hint: Stream the model's reasoning into the progress message (extended-thinking models). Off by default — it's usually noise.

configSchema.label: Service language
configSchema.hint: Language of the bot's own service messages (/new, /model, placeholders): en (default) or cs.

configSchema.label: Conversation
configSchema.hint: Image routing.

configSchema.label: Vision model
configSchema.hint: Model used for turns that carry image attachments (must support vision). Empty = the chat's normal model.

configSchema.label: Media limits
configSchema.hint: Caps on inbound/outbound images and how long a pending question stays open.

configSchema.label: Max inbound image size
configSchema.hint: Largest image attachment (bytes) the bot downloads for vision; bigger ones are noted instead.

configSchema.label: Max images per message
configSchema.hint: How many image attachments in a single incoming message the bot sends to the vision model.

configSchema.label: Max images per reply
configSchema.hint: How many generated images the bot attaches to a single outgoing reply.

configSchema.label: Question timeout (ms)
configSchema.hint: How long a pending ask_user_question stays open for a numbered reply or free text before it's dropped as stale.

configSchema.label: Sender policies
configSchema.hint: Map WhatsApp senders to allowed projects, a role prompt and the tools the bot may use.

configSchema.label: Sender policies
configSchema.hint: First matching sender wins; senders with no mapped policy are ignored. The ID is a phone number (420777123456), a JID (…@s.whatsapp.net) or a whole group JID (…@g.us) — a group ID grants access to everyone in it. Mark a policy admin to let it switch the model and use the group tools. Each policy may also restrict which tools the bot can use for it.
